data.text: I’ve confirmed the duplication is real and concentrated in two files. Next I’m checking the surrounding package structure so the shared leaf package can stay dependency-free and not introduce an import cycle.
I’ve narrowed the extraction points. I’m reading the tests now so the shared package keeps the same semantics and the existing assertions stay valid after the move.

data.output:
package agent

import (
	"context"

	agenttry "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent/try"
)

func toTryReport(report ExecuteBeadReport) agenttry.Report {
	cycleTrace := make([]agenttry.ExecutionCycleTrace, 0, len(report.CycleTrace))
	for _, entry := range report.CycleTrace {
		cycleTrace = append(cycleTrace, toTryCycleTrace(entry))
	}
	return agenttry.Report{
		BeadID:                      report.BeadID,
		AttemptID:                   report.AttemptID,
		WorkerID:                    report.WorkerID,
		Harness:                     report.Harness,
		Provider:                    report.Provider,
		Model:                       report.Model,
		ActualPower:                 report.ActualPower,
		Status:                      report.Status,
		Detail:                      report.Detail,
		SessionID:                   report.SessionID,
		BaseRev:                     report.BaseRev,
		ResultRev:                   report.ResultRev,
		ImplementationRev:           report.ImplementationRev,
		LandedRev:                   report.LandedRev,
		TargetBranch:                report.TargetBranch,
		EvidenceRev:                 report.EvidenceRev,
		ProjectRoot:                 report.ProjectRoot,
		PreserveRef:                 report.PreserveRef,
		RetryAfter:                  report.RetryAfter,
		NoChangesRationale:          report.NoChangesRationale,
		ReviewVerdict:               report.ReviewVerdict,
		ReviewRationale:             report.ReviewRationale,
		CycleTrace:                  cycleTrace,
		PowerClass:                  report.PowerClass,
		ProbeResult:                 report.ProbeResult,
		CostUSD:                     report.CostUSD,
		DurationMS:                  report.DurationMS,
		RequestedProfile:            report.RequestedProfile,
		RoutingIntentSource:         report.RoutingIntentSource,
		EstimatedDifficulty:         report.EstimatedDifficulty,
		InferredPowerClass:          report.InferredPowerClass,
		ResolvedPowerClass:          report.ResolvedPowerClass,
		EscalationCount:             report.EscalationCount,
		FinalPowerClass:             report.FinalPowerClass,
		DecompositionRecommendation: report.DecompositionRecommendation,
		DecompositionRationale:      report.DecompositionRationale,
		Disrupted:                   report.Disrupted,
		DisruptionReason:            report.DisruptionReason,
		OutcomeReason:               report.OutcomeReason,
		ResourceExhausted:           report.ResourceExhausted,
		Error:                       report.Error,
		Stderr:                      report.Stderr,
		RateLimitBudget:             report.RateLimitBudget,
	}
}

func fromTryReport(report agenttry.Report) ExecuteBeadReport {
	cycleTrace := make([]ExecutionCycleTrace, 0, len(report.CycleTrace))
	for _, entry := range report.CycleTrace {
		cycleTrace = append(cycleTrace, fromTryCycleTrace(entry))
	}
	return ExecuteBeadReport{
		BeadID:                      report.BeadID,
		AttemptID:                   report.AttemptID,
		WorkerID:                    report.WorkerID,
		Harness:                     report.Harness,
		Provider:                    report.Provider,
		Model:                       report.Model,
		ActualPower:                 report.ActualPower,
		Status:                      report.Status,
		Detail:                      report.Detail,
		SessionID:                   report.SessionID,
		BaseRev:                     report.BaseRev,
		ResultRev:                   report.ResultRev,
		ImplementationRev:           report.ImplementationRev,
		LandedRev:                   report.LandedRev,
		TargetBranch:                report.TargetBranch,
		EvidenceRev:                 report.EvidenceRev,
		ProjectRoot:                 report.ProjectRoot,
		PreserveRef:                 report.PreserveRef,
		RetryAfter:                  report.RetryAfter,
		NoChangesRationale:          report.NoChangesRationale,
		ReviewVerdict:               report.ReviewVerdict,
		ReviewRationale:             report.ReviewRationale,
		CycleTrace:                  cycleTrace,
		PowerClass:                  report.PowerClass,
		ProbeResult:                 report.ProbeResult,
		CostUSD:                     report.CostUSD,
		DurationMS:                  report.DurationMS,
		RequestedProfile:            report.RequestedProfile,
		RoutingIntentSource:         report.RoutingIntentSource,
		EstimatedDifficulty:         report.EstimatedDifficulty,
		InferredPowerClass:          report.InferredPowerClass,
		ResolvedPowerClass:          report.ResolvedPowerClass,
		EscalationCount:             report.EscalationCount,
		FinalPowerClass:             report.FinalPowerClass,
		DecompositionRecommendation: report.DecompositionRecommendation,
		DecompositionRationale:      report.DecompositionRationale,
		Disrupted:                   report.Disrupted,
		DisruptionReason:            report.DisruptionReason,
		OutcomeReason:               report.OutcomeReason,
		ResourceExhausted:           report.ResourceExhausted,
		Error:                       report.Error,
		Stderr:                      report.Stderr,
		RateLimitBudget:             report.RateLimitBudget,
	}
}

func fromTryRateLimitRetryInfo(info agenttry.RateLimitRetryInfo) RateLimitRetryInfo {
	var result *Result
	if info.Report != nil {
		result = &Result{
			Harness:  info.Report.Harness,
			Provider: info.Report.Provider,
			Model:    info.Report.Model,
		}

data.text: I’ve got the main design choice to settle: keep the package-specific hook payloads local, while moving the pure policy to a shared leaf package. I’m counting the literal call sites now so I can pick the least risky shape for the wrapper types before editing.

I’ve settled on the wrapper shape: shared pure policy in a new leaf package, with agent/try keeping their request-specific payloads local but delegating the classification and backoff math. I’m patching the shared package first, then I’ll switch the two callers over and clean up the tests.
The first pass of the refactor is in; I’m now doing a compile-sanity sweep for leftover references and constructor sites that need nested literals after the wrapper change.

package agent

import (
	"context"

	agenttry "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent/try"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/ratelimitpolicy"
)

func toTryReport(report ExecuteBeadReport) agenttry.Report {
	cycleTrace := make([]agenttry.ExecutionCycleTrace, 0, len(report.CycleTrace))
	for _, entry := range report.CycleTrace {
		cycleTrace = append(cycleTrace, toTryCycleTrace(entry))
	}
	return agenttry.Report{
		BeadID:                      report.BeadID,
		AttemptID:                   report.AttemptID,
		WorkerID:                    report.WorkerID,
		Harness:                     report.Harness,
		Provider:                    report.Provider,
		Model:                       report.Model,
		ActualPower:                 report.ActualPower,
		Status:                      report.Status,
		Detail:                      report.Detail,
		SessionID:                   report.SessionID,
		BaseRev:                     report.BaseRev,
		ResultRev:                   report.ResultRev,
		ImplementationRev:           report.ImplementationRev,
		LandedRev:                   report.LandedRev,
		TargetBranch:                report.TargetBranch,
		EvidenceRev:                 report.EvidenceRev,
		ProjectRoot:                 report.ProjectRoot,
		PreserveRef:                 report.PreserveRef,
		RetryAfter:                  report.RetryAfter,
		NoChangesRationale:          report.NoChangesRationale,
		ReviewVerdict:               report.ReviewVerdict,
		ReviewRationale:             report.ReviewRationale,
		CycleTrace:                  cycleTrace,
		PowerClass:                  report.PowerClass,
		ProbeResult:                 report.ProbeResult,
		CostUSD:                     report.CostUSD,
		DurationMS:                  report.DurationMS,
		RequestedProfile:            report.RequestedProfile,
		RoutingIntentSource:         report.RoutingIntentSource,
		EstimatedDifficulty:         report.EstimatedDifficulty,
		InferredPowerClass:          report.InferredPowerClass,
		ResolvedPowerClass:          report.ResolvedPowerClass,
		EscalationCount:             report.EscalationCount,
		FinalPowerClass:             report.FinalPowerClass,
		DecompositionRecommendation: report.DecompositionRecommendation,
		DecompositionRationale:      report.DecompositionRationale,
		Disrupted:                   report.Disrupted,
		DisruptionReason:            report.DisruptionReason,
		OutcomeReason:               report.OutcomeReason,
		ResourceExhausted:           report.ResourceExhausted,
		Error:                       report.Error,
		Stderr:                      report.Stderr,
		RateLimitBudget:             report.RateLimitBudget,
	}
}

func fromTryReport(report agenttry.Report) ExecuteBeadReport {
	cycleTrace := make([]ExecutionCycleTrace, 0, len(report.CycleTrace))
	for _, entry := range report.CycleTrace {
		cycleTrace = append(cycleTrace, fromTryCycleTrace(entry))
	}
	return ExecuteBeadReport{
		BeadID:                      report.BeadID,
		AttemptID:                   report.AttemptID,
		WorkerID:                    report.WorkerID,
		Harness:                     report.Harness,
		Provider:                    report.Provider,
		Model:                       report.Model,
		ActualPower:                 report.ActualPower,
		Status:                      report.Status,
		Detail:                      report.Detail,
		SessionID:                   report.SessionID,
		BaseRev:                     report.BaseRev,
		ResultRev:                   report.ResultRev,
		ImplementationRev:           report.ImplementationRev,
		LandedRev:                   report.LandedRev,
		TargetBranch:                report.TargetBranch,
		EvidenceRev:                 report.EvidenceRev,
		ProjectRoot:                 report.ProjectRoot,
		PreserveRef:                 report.PreserveRef,
		RetryAfter:                  report.RetryAfter,
		NoChangesRationale:          report.NoChangesRationale,
		ReviewVerdict:               report.ReviewVerdict,
		ReviewRationale:             report.ReviewRationale,
		CycleTrace:                  cycleTrace,
		PowerClass:                  report.PowerClass,
		ProbeResult:                 report.ProbeResult,
		CostUSD:                     report.CostUSD,
		DurationMS:                  report.DurationMS,
		RequestedProfile:            report.RequestedProfile,
		RoutingIntentSource:         report.RoutingIntentSource,
		EstimatedDifficulty:         report.EstimatedDifficulty,
		InferredPowerClass:          report.InferredPowerClass,
		ResolvedPowerClass:          report.ResolvedPowerClass,
		EscalationCount:             report.EscalationCount,
		FinalPowerClass:             report.FinalPowerClass,
		DecompositionRecommendation: report.DecompositionRecommendation,
		DecompositionRationale:      report.DecompositionRationale,
		Disrupted:                   report.Disrupted,
		DisruptionReason:            report.DisruptionReason,
		OutcomeReason:               report.OutcomeReason,
		ResourceExhausted:           report.ResourceExhausted,
		Error:                       report.Error,
		Stderr:                      report.Stderr,
		RateLimitBudget:             report.RateLimitBudget,
	}
}

func fromTryRateLimitRetryInfo(info agenttry.RateLimitRetryInfo) RateLimitRetryInfo {
	var result *Result
	if info.Report != nil {
		result = &Result{
			Harness:  info.Report.Harness,
			Provider: info.Report.Provider,
			Model:    info.Report.Model,
		}
	}
	return RateLimitRetryInfo{
		RateLimitRetryInfo: ratelimitpolicy.RateLimitRetryInfo{
			Attempt:    info.Attempt,
			Wait:       info.Wait,
			Source:     info.Source,
			Elapsed:    info.Elapsed,
			OverBudget: info.OverBudget,
		},
		Result: result,
	}
}

func toTryCycleTrace(entry ExecutionCycleTrace) agenttry.ExecutionCycleTrace {
	return agenttry.ExecutionCycleTrace{
		CycleIndex: entry.CycleIndex,
		AttemptID:  entry.AttemptID,
		ResultRev:  entry.ResultRev,
		ImplementerRoute: agenttry.ExecutionCycleRouteFacts{
			Harness:         entry.ImplementerRoute.Harness,
			Provider:        entry.ImplementerRoute.Provider,
			Model:           entry.ImplementerRoute.Model,
			ActualPower:     entry.ImplementerRoute.ActualPower,
			RouteReason:     entry.ImplementerRoute.RouteReason,
			ResolvedBaseURL: entry.ImplementerRoute.ResolvedBaseURL,
		},
		ReviewGroupID:   entry.ReviewGroupID,
		ReviewerIndices: append([]int(nil), entry.ReviewerIndices...),
		ReviewVerdicts:  append([]string(nil), entry.ReviewVerdicts...),
		ReviewResult: agenttry.ExecutionCycleReviewResult{
			Verdict:        entry.ReviewResult.Verdict,
			Rationale:      entry.ReviewResult.Rationale,
			Classification: entry.ReviewResult.Classification,
			PerAC:          toTryReviewACs(entry.ReviewResult.PerAC),
			Findings:       toTryFindings(entry.ReviewResult.Findings),
		},
		FinalDecision: entry.FinalDecision,
	}
}

1	package agent
     2	
     3	import (
     4		"context"
     5		"strings"
     6		"time"
     7	
     8		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/ratelimitpolicy"
     9		agentlib "github.com/easel/fizeau"
    10	)
    11	
    12	// RateLimitRetryDefaultBudget is the default per-bead total wait budget for
    13	// rate-limit retries. AC #4 of ddx-c6e3db02 names 5 minutes.
    14	const RateLimitRetryDefaultBudget = ratelimitpolicy.RateLimitRetryDefaultBudget
    15	
    16	// RateLimitRetryDefaultPerWaitCap caps any single retry wait so a misbehaving
    17	// provider cannot pin the worker with a one-hour Retry-After. The bead
    18	// description names 60 seconds as the per-wait cap.
    19	const RateLimitRetryDefaultPerWaitCap = ratelimitpolicy.RateLimitRetryDefaultPerWaitCap
    20	
    21	// RateLimitBudgetExhaustedReason is the canonical reason string written into
    22	// Result.Error when the per-bead retry budget is exhausted. The execute-bead
    23	// status mapping translates this into FailureModeAuthError so it surfaces in
    24	// the standard execution_failed pathway (TD-031 §8.4).
    25	const RateLimitBudgetExhaustedReason = ratelimitpolicy.RateLimitBudgetExhaustedReason
    26	
    27	// RateLimitRetryEventKind is the bead event kind appended on each retry per
    28	// TD-031 §4 / §8.4 RateLimitRetryContract. The body carries the retry count
    29	// and wait duration.
    30	const RateLimitRetryEventKind = "rate-limit-retry"
    31	
    32	// The shared backoff schedule is the exponential-backoff fallback when a 429
    33	// response carries no parseable Retry-After value. It names the wait for the
    34	// 1st, 2nd, … retry; entries past the end repeat the last value. AC #3 of
    35	// ddx-c6e3db02 specifies 1s, 5s, 15s, 30s, 60s.
    36	type RateLimitWaitDecision = ratelimitpolicy.RateLimitWaitDecision
    37	
    38	// EvaluateRateLimitWait decides how long to wait before retrying after a
    39	// rate-limit response, and whether the per-bead budget allows the retry.
    40	//
    41	// retryAfter is the duration parsed from the response (zero when absent /
    42	// unparseable). attempt is 1-indexed (first retry = 1). elapsed is the total
    43	// wait already spent on this bead. budget is the total cap (zero = unbounded,
    44	// disabling budget enforcement). perWaitCap caps any single wait (zero =
    45	// uncapped).
    46	//
    47	// The function is pure: no I/O, no time.Now, no global state.
    48	func EvaluateRateLimitWait(retryAfter time.Duration, attempt int, elapsed, budget, perWaitCap time.Duration) RateLimitWaitDecision {
    49		return ratelimitpolicy.EvaluateRateLimitWait(retryAfter, attempt, elapsed, budget, perWaitCap)
    50	}
    51	
    52	// IsRateLimitResult reports whether a Runner Result indicates a rate-limit
    53	// (HTTP 429) event that the per-bead retry policy should handle.
    54	//
    55	// Quota exhaustion (NoViableProviderForNow) is OUT of scope for
    56	// RateLimitRetryContract per ddx-c6e3db02 AC #8 — it belongs to
    57	// QuotaPauseContract (ddx-aede917d). The detection here matches the canonical
    58	// 429 / "rate limit" / "ratelimit" markers without matching "quota exceeded".
    59	func IsRateLimitResult(result *Result) bool {
    60		if result == nil {
    61			return false
    62		}
    63		combined := strings.ToLower(result.Error + "\n" + result.Stderr)
    64		return ratelimitpolicy.IsRateLimitText(combined)
    65	}
    66	
    67	// ParseRetryAfter parses a Retry-After header value per RFC 7231 §7.1.3:
    68	// either a non-negative integer ("delta-seconds") or an HTTP-date.
    69	// Returns zero duration when the value is empty or unparseable.
    70	//
    71	// now is supplied so tests can pin the clock; production callers pass
    72	// time.Now().
    73	func ParseRetryAfter(value string, now time.Time) time.Duration {
    74		return ratelimitpolicy.ParseRetryAfter(value, now)
    75	}
    76	
    77	// ExtractRetryAfterFromStderr scans subprocess stderr for a Retry-After
    78	// indication and returns the parsed wait. Returns zero when no indication is
    79	// present so the caller falls back to exponential backoff.
    80	//
    81	// Recognised forms (case-insensitive):
    82	//   - "Retry-After: <value>" (HTTP header echoed by the harness)
    83	//   - "retry_after=<value>" (structured key=value form some harnesses emit)
    84	//   - "retry after <value>"
    85	//
    86	// now is used as the reference for HTTP-date values.
    87	func ExtractRetryAfterFromStderr(stderr string, now time.Time) time.Duration {
    88		return ratelimitpolicy.ExtractRetryAfterFromStderr(stderr, now)
    89	}
    90	
    91	// RateLimitRetryConfig configures the retry wrapper. Zero values mean
    92	// "use the package default".
    93	type RateLimitRetryConfig struct {
    94		ratelimitpolicy.RateLimitRetryConfig
    95		// OnRetry, when non-nil, is invoked after each wait + before the retry
    96		// attempt fires. Used by integration to emit the rate-limit-retry bead
    97		// event and the routing-engine RecordRouteAttempt feedback.
    98		OnRetry func(ctx context.Context, info RateLimitRetryInfo)
    99	}
   100	
   101	// RateLimitRetryInfo describes one rate-limit retry event for telemetry hooks.
   102	type RateLimitRetryInfo struct {
   103		ratelimitpolicy.RateLimitRetryInfo
   104		Result *Result
   105	}
   106	
   107	// resolved returns a copy of cfg with zero fields filled in from defaults.
   108	func (cfg RateLimitRetryConfig) resolved() RateLimitRetryConfig {
   109		out := cfg
   110		if out.Budget == 0 {
   111			out.Budget = RateLimitRetryDefaultBudget
   112		}
   113		if out.PerWaitCap == 0 {
   114			out.PerWaitCap = RateLimitRetryDefaultPerWaitCap
   115		}
   116		if out.Sleep == nil {
   117			out.Sleep = ctxSleep
   118		}
   119		if out.Now == nil {
   120			out.Now = time.Now
   121		}
   122		return out
   123	}
   124	
   125	func ctxSleep(ctx context.Context, d time.Duration) error {
   126		if d <= 0 {
   127			return nil
   128		}
   129		t := time.NewTimer(d)
   130		defer t.Stop()
   131		select {
   132		case <-ctx.Done():
   133			return ctx.Err()
   134		case <-t.C:
   135			return nil
   136		}
   137	}
   138	
   139	// RunWithRateLimitRetry invokes attempt and, when its result is a rate-limit
   140	// signal per IsRateLimitResult, waits per EvaluateRateLimitWait and retries
   141	// until either attempt returns a non-rate-limit result or the per-bead budget
   142	// is exhausted. Budget exhaustion mutates the final result's Error to
   143	// RateLimitBudgetExhaustedReason and returns it without further retries.
   144	//
   145	// The wrapper does NOT change provider availability state — the provider
   146	// stays in rotation per AC #2 of ddx-c6e3db02. Per AC #6, callers wire
   147	// RecordRouteAttempt via OnRetry for transparency.
   148	func RunWithRateLimitRetry(ctx context.Context, cfg RateLimitRetryConfig, attempt func(ctx context.Context) (*Result, error)) (*Result, error) {
   149		if cfg.Budget < 0 {
   150			// Negative budget disables the wrapper entirely (parity with
   151			// "feature off"). Useful for tests and operator opt-out.
   152			return attempt(ctx)
   153		}
   154		cfg = cfg.resolved()
   155	
   156		var (
   157			elapsed    time.Duration
   158			retryCount int
   159		)
   160		for {
   161			res, err := attempt(ctx)
   162			if err != nil || res == nil {
   163				return res, err
   164			}
   165			if !IsRateLimitResult(res) {
   166				return res, nil
   167			}
   168	
   169			retryCount++
   170			retryAfter := ExtractRetryAfterFromStderr(res.Stderr, cfg.Now())
   171			decision := EvaluateRateLimitWait(retryAfter, retryCount, elapsed, cfg.Budget, cfg.PerWaitCap)
   172			if !decision.ShouldRetry {
   173				// Budget exhausted: surface the canonical reason so the standard
   174				// execution_failed mapping fires (TD-031 §5).
   175				res.Error = RateLimitBudgetExhaustedReason
   176				if cfg.OnRetry != nil {
   177					cfg.OnRetry(ctx, RateLimitRetryInfo{
   178						RateLimitRetryInfo: ratelimitpolicy.RateLimitRetryInfo{
   179							Attempt:    retryCount,
   180							Source:     decision.Source,
   181							Elapsed:    elapsed,
   182							OverBudget: true,
   183						},
   184						Result: res,
   185					})
   186				}
   187				return res, nil
   188			}
   189	
   190			if cfg.OnRetry != nil {
   191				cfg.OnRetry(ctx, RateLimitRetryInfo{
   192					RateLimitRetryInfo: ratelimitpolicy.RateLimitRetryInfo{
   193						Attempt: retryCount,
   194						Wait:    decision.Wait,
   195						Source:  decision.Source,
   196						Elapsed: elapsed,
   197					},
   198					Result: res,
   199				})
   200			}
   201	
   202			if err := cfg.Sleep(ctx, decision.Wait); err != nil {
   203				return res, err
   204			}
   205			elapsed += decision.Wait
   206		}
   207	}
   208	
   209	// BuildRateLimitRouteAttempt constructs a fizeau.RouteAttempt that records a
   210	// rate-limit retry on the routing-engine feedback channel. The Status is
   211	// "rate_limited" so the engine has signal even though provider availability
   212	// remains unchanged per RateLimitRetryContract.
   213	func BuildRateLimitRouteAttempt(info RateLimitRetryInfo) agentlib.RouteAttempt {
   214		att := agentlib.RouteAttempt{
   215			Status:    "rate_limited",
   216			Reason:    info.Source,
   217			Duration:  info.Wait,
   218			Timestamp: time.Now().UTC(),
   219		}
   220		if info.Result != nil {

Committed as `7e812e08b` (`refactor: share rate-limit policy between agent and try [ddx-73d2279d]`).

What changed:
- Moved the shared rate-limit policy, constants, and parse/classify logic into [`cli/internal/ratelimitpolicy/ratelimitpolicy.go`](/Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-73d2279d-20260607T161041-24a6fbc7/cli/internal/ratelimitpolicy/ratelimitpolicy.go#L1).
- Updated [`cli/internal/agent/ratelimit_retry.go`](/Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-73d2279d-20260607T161041-24a6fbc7/cli/internal/agent/ratelimit_retry.go#L12) to wrap the shared policy for `Result`-typed handling.
- Updated [`cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go`](/Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-73d2279d-20260607T161041-24a6fbc7/cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go#L441) to delegate to the shared policy and removed the local parse helpers.
- Adjusted the adapter/tests to the new embedded policy types in [`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_try_adapter.go`](/Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-73d2279d-20260607T161041-24a6fbc7/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_try_adapter.go#L113) and [`cli/internal/agent/ratelimit_retry_test.go`](/Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-73d2279d-20260607T161041-24a6fbc7/cli/internal/agent/ratelimit_retry_test.go#L198).

Verification:
- `go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/agent/try/...`
- `lefthook run pre-commit`
